name: kpi-dashboard-design
description: Design effective KPI dashboards with metrics selection, visualization best practices, and real-time monitoring patterns. Use when building business dashboards, selecting metrics, or designing data visualization layouts.
capabilities: Apply KPI framework (strategic, tactical, operational levels) to organize metrics
Design dashboard layouts with executive summary, department views, and drill-downs
Select appropriate KPIs by department (sales, marketing, product, finance)
Create SQL queries for common KPI calculations (MRR, ARR, retention, CAC)
Implement real-time monitoring displays with alerts and thresholds
Follow visualization best practices (color, spacing, chart selection)
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The skill contains markdown documentation with code examples (SQL, Python, YAML). The backtick characters flagged as 'external_commands' are markdown syntax for code blocks, not shell execution. External URLs are reference links to educational resources (dashboard design articles), not network requests. No actual security risks identified.
valueStatement: Create effective KPI dashboards that clearly communicate business performance. This skill provides frameworks, visualization patterns, and SQL templates for building executive, departmental, and operational dashboards.
userTitle: Design KPI Dashboards That Drive Decisions
limitations: Does not generate full working code (provides patterns and examples only)
Does not connect to specific data sources or databases
Does not provide deployment or infrastructure guidance
Does not cover advanced ML-based forecasting or anomaly detection

useCases.targetUser: Business analyst or data team lead
useCases.title: Build Executive Dashboard
useCases.description: Design a high-level executive summary showing 4-6 headline KPIs with trends and alerts for C-suite stakeholders.

useCases.title: Create Department-Specific Views
useCases.description: Develop tailored dashboards for sales, marketing, product, or finance teams with relevant metrics and visualizations.

useCases.title: Implement Real-Time Operations Monitor
useCases.description: Build an operations center dashboard showing system health, throughput, error rates, and live alerts.

promptTemplates.title: Get KPI Framework
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me understand the KPI framework for dashboard design. I need to know the three levels (strategic, tactical, operational) and when to use each.
promptTemplates.scenario: Understanding the foundational framework before designing a dashboard

promptTemplates.title: Select Sales KPIs
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the most important KPIs for a sales dashboard? Include revenue metrics, pipeline metrics, and activity metrics with brief explanations.

promptTemplates.title: Design Executive Summary Layout
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me a layout pattern for an executive summary dashboard. Include suggested KPIs, visualization types, and how to present trends and alerts.

promptTemplates.title: Write MRR SQL Query
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a SQL query to calculate Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR) from a subscriptions table. Include month-over-month growth calculation.
promptTemplates.scenario: Implementing KPI calculations in a database

bestPractices: Limit the dashboard to 5-7 KPIs to avoid overwhelming the audience
Show context with comparisons (previous period, target, benchmark)
Use consistent color coding: green for positive trends, red for negative
antiPatterns: Including vanity metrics that look impressive but do not drive decisions
Overcrowding the dashboard with too many charts and tables
Using 3D charts or decorative elements that distort data perception

faq.question: What is the difference between strategic, tactical, and operational KPIs?
faq.answer: Strategic KPIs track long-term goals (monthly/quarterly) for executives. Tactical KPIs measure department goals (weekly/monthly) for managers. Operational KPIs monitor day-to-day activities (real-time/daily) for teams.

faq.question: How many KPIs should be on a single dashboard?
faq.answer: Aim for 5-7 KPIs on any single view. Executive summaries should be even more concise (4-6). Use drill-downs for detailed analysis.

faq.question: What are SMART KPIs?
faq.answer: SMART stands for Specific (clear definition), Measurable (quantifiable), Achievable (realistic targets), Relevant (aligned to goals), and Time-bound (defined period).

faq.question: How often should dashboard KPIs be updated?
faq.answer: Match update frequency to the KPI type: operational (real-time/daily), tactical (weekly/monthly), strategic (monthly/quarterly). Do not refresh faster than data accuracy allows.

faq.question: What is a good LTV/CAC ratio?
faq.answer: A healthy LTV/CAC ratio is typically 3:1 or higher. This means customer lifetime value is at least three times the cost to acquire them.

faq.question: How do I choose the right chart type for my KPI?
faq.answer: Use line charts for trends over time. Use bar charts for comparisons across categories. Use pie charts for part-to-whole (limited slices). Use gauges for current status against targets.
